response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer, but he does not meet the mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ experience is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project, falling far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His primary professional stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain experience — the one stint at RIDE Capital involved investment tooling but in TypeScript/GraphQL, not C++. SQL Server and Oracle are absent from his profile, with only general SQL and MySQL mentioned.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and clean code practices demonstrated across multiple roles
Broad language exposure including C++, Java, and scripting languages, indicating adaptability and ability to ramp up
Some financial-adjacent domain exposure (RIDE Capital — investment platform) showing familiarity with regulated, transactional environments
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professional) versus the mandatory 5–10 years required, and no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge — the core domain requirement is entirely unmet
No experience with SQL Server or Oracle; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL, which are irrelevant to the target stack

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
